document_id: Interactive Digital Media Credits (Article 3F) Processed During Calendar Year 2012

findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated boilerplate, styling/theme content, and document metadata not present in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML contains text not shown in the PDF such as '<title>Converted Document</title>', CSS comment 'Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS', and imported font/theme identifiers like 'Source Sans 3', '--nc-navy', '--nc-blue', etc. The PDF only shows the revenue report content and does not contain this textual metadata/theme content.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is not faithful to the document structure because the PDF presents four centered title lines of similar title styling, while the HTML imposes a nested h1/h2/h3 plus paragraph structure.
findings.evidence:
PDF title block: 'North Carolina Department of Revenue' / 'Technology Development Credits Taken' / 'Interactive Digital Media Credits (Article 3F)' / 'Processed during Calendar Year 2012'. HTML maps these as '

North Carolina Department of Revenue

Technology Development Credits Taken

Interactive Digital Media Credits (Article 3F)

Processed during Calendar Year 2012

', which adds a semantic hierarchy not clearly present in the PDF and demotes the fourth title line to body text.
